fieldValues.value: RFC-017 Change E: Soft size-warning conformance

When an `attachment_policy` is present, an implementation MAY enforce its limits as hard rejections. If it does not enforce them as hard limits, it MUST surface violations as non-blocking warning diagnostics at `srs repo validate` time: when a source document content file exceeds `maxPerFileBytes` or `maxDocBytes`; when aggregate source-document bytes exceed `maxTotalBytes`; when an attached file's MIME type is not listed in `allowedMimeTypes`. Non-blocking diagnostics MUST NOT prevent record storage or repository export. Whether to emit a warning at write time (e.g. `srs source import`) is implementation-defined; conformance is required at validate time. Hard caps are deferred to a future extension.

Hard size caps at the spec level would set arbitrary limits that break legitimate use cases (e.g., video-heavy documentary repositories). Soft warnings surfaced as diagnostics give operators visibility without blocking valid workflows; the application layer or a future hard-cap extension can enforce stricter limits.
